regime: Conflicted Trend, Distribution Session (Long Signal on a 15-Point Leash)
summary:
The Nasdaq handed you 424 points overnight and took every one of them back. MNQ ran to 29,192.50 before the open, printed a 29,118.50 RTH high, then faded for six straight hours to close 28,777.75, up 9.00 points on the day. Flat. The whole thing was a distribution.

The number that matters: MNQ closed 15.34 points above its own 1hr trailing stop at 28,762.41. That stop ratcheted up 162 points today while price went nowhere, so the cushion that was 168 points on Friday is essentially gone.

MES never played along. Its stop stayed short at 7,534.22 and price closed 50.72 below it, a third straight session with the indexes pointing different directions. Gold coiled into the tightest RTH range in weeks, sigma 1.95, low printing exactly 4,000.00. Crude ran 84.20 to 79.55 and back to 82.44 and flipped its trend stop twice inside 24 hours.
psychology:
Today punished conviction and rewarded rules, which is the least satisfying combination there is.

If you bought the overnight strength you were right for eleven hours and flat by the close. If you were still holding at 20:00 UTC with no level to lean on, the only thing that decided your day was luck. An overnight gap is inventory. It is somebody's position, not a trend, and it has to be defended in RTH before it means anything. Today it was not.

The practical version: before you take a gap, write down the price where you are wrong. Not a feeling, a number. On MNQ that number was sitting right there at 28,762.41, and it was the difference between a scratch and a slow bleed. The trade you can define is the trade you can survive.

event.name: No scheduled release (Monday July 20 carried no major US macro print)
event.surprise: None scheduled. Today was positioning rather than data.
event.nqReaction: MNQ gapped to a 29,192.50 overnight high, printed a 29,118.50 RTH high, then faded six consecutive hours to close 28,777.75, up 9.00 (+0.03%). A full round trip with no catalyst behind either leg.
event.crossAsset: Crude ran an 84.20 to 79.55 to 82.44 overnight round trip on geopolitical headlines with no economic print behind it, the second consecutive session where energy moved on headlines. Gold compressed to a 23.90-point RTH range. MES closed -0.15% and never reclaimed its stop. Next scheduled catalysts are Friday's July flash PMIs and June New Home Sales; the larger swing factor is this week's Alphabet, Tesla, Intel and IBM earnings block, which no economic calendar carries.

observe.name: ES/NQ confirmation
observe.note: Third straight session, same polarity as Friday: MNQ long, MES short. Three breaks in four sessions means the index complex has no working agreement, and that is the single most useful thing on this page.

observe.name: GAP_NQ
observe.note: Outside the Net Alpha Pro stack. Context only.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ
observe.note: Outside the Net Alpha Pro stack. Context only.

risk.ddProximity: Not binding. Book entered flat, full daily budget intact.
risk.sizing: Reduced 25 to 50% per the REDUCED band. MNQ ATR(14) fell to 132.28 from 143.96, so the volatility input allows slightly more contracts. Do not take it. The binding constraint is signal quality, not volatility. Lines that must hold: MNQ 28,762.41 / MES 7,534.22 as resistance / MGC 3,990.15 / MCL 80.49.
risk.openRisk: No correlated stack. MNQ, MGC and MCL are long-signal, MES is short-signal, and MCL flipped twice today, so nothing here confirms anything else.
risk.note: De-escalated from DEFENSIVE. Friday's escalation was driven by weekend gap risk, which resolved without damage, so it comes off. What replaces it is a different problem: MNQ's long signal sits 15.34 points, 0.05% of price, above its own invalidation while the correlated index points short. That is one REDUCED trigger, not two. Week ahead: no major US release until Friday's July flash PMIs and June New Home Sales at 9:45 and 10:00 ET. The real risk is the earnings block, with Alphabet, Tesla, Intel and IBM reporting into a Nasdaq that just failed a 424-point overnight rally. Earnings gaps do not respect stops. Size positions you would be willing to hold through one.

setups.entry: OR break down with no reclaim, continuation below NYVWAP
setups.bias: In habitat. Textbook on MES today: broke down 13:50 UTC at 7,528.50, no reclaim, trended to a 7,483.50 close.

setups.name: TSMOM-20
setups.bias: Borderline. Long signal exists but MNQ is 15.34 points from invalidation and MES points the other way.

setups.name: AVWAP Deviation Band
setups.entry: Band tag at +/-2s from the 28,917.40 AVWAP
setups.bias: Borderline. Close at -1.60s, between bands. Upper band produced a reversion today; -2s at 28,743.08 is the next actionable tag.

setups.name: AVWAP Midrange
setups.entry: Range play between AVWAP and OR boundary, 9:30 to 10:30 ET
setups.bias: Borderline. The MNQ opening range was 156.25 wide, so playable structure but wide risk.

tomorrowsMap.if: MNQ closes below 28,762.41 on 2 hourly closes
tomorrowsMap.then: The long flip is done and the 28,750.00 RTH low, the 28,743.08 -2s and then the 28,655.92 -3s come into scope, with 28,500 psych behind them
tomorrowsMap.bias: Short-side under 28,762.41. The cushion is 15.34 points, 0.05% of price, so this is the single most likely first event of the session

tomorrowsMap.if: MNQ reclaims the 28,917.40 AVWAP on 2 hourly closes
tomorrowsMap.then: The fade is repaired and the 28,966.97 NYVWAP, the 29,004.56 +1s and the 29,000 psych are the tests, with 29,118.50 the level that would actually prove it
tomorrowsMap.bias: Long-side only above 28,917.40. Between the stop and the AVWAP there is no trend, only range

tomorrowsMap.if: MES closes above 7,534.22 on 2 hourly closes
tomorrowsMap.then: The three-session disagreement ends and both indexes finally point the same way, with the 7,545.48 +3s and the 7,552.25 overnight high as references
tomorrowsMap.bias: While MES sits below 7,534.22 and MNQ sits below 28,917.40, both trend layers actually agree on down, and that is the cleanest agreement in three sessions

tomorrowsMap.if: MGC closes below 4,000 on 2 hourly closes
tomorrowsMap.then: The coil resolves lower and the 3,990.15 stop then the 3,986.60 overnight low are the references
tomorrowsMap.bias: Two-sided by design. RTH sigma of 1.95 is the tightest in weeks. Compression resolves but nothing says which way, so wait for the break. Above: 4,023.90 then 4,046.10 then 4,050

tomorrowsMap.if: MCL holds above the 80.49 stop on hourly closes
tomorrowsMap.then: The second long flip in 24 hours stands and the 83.16 RTH high then the 83.35 +3s are the upside references
tomorrowsMap.bias: Treat crude's trend layer as unusable. It flipped twice in one session and ran a 4.65-dollar overnight round trip. Size for headline gap risk or leave it alone

yesterdaysMapScore.note: Three triggers, zero clean grades. Rows 1, 4 and 5 each did the right thing and then undid it inside the same session. Rows 2 and 3 did not trigger. That is not a mapping failure, it is what a round-trip session does to conditional levels.

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked
fullReport.html:
#Row (as published Jul 17)StatusGradeWhat happened
1MNQ reclaims the 28,843.93 AVWAP on 2 hourly closesTRIGGEREDMIXEDReclaimed on the 23:00 UTC bar, confirmed next close, then held twelve straight hourly closes above the line from 07:00 UTC. Every listed target printed: 29,000 psych, the 29,006.57 +2s and the 29,047 RTH high were all cleared, topping at 29,192.50 overnight and 29,118.50 in RTH. Then it round-tripped. Close 28,777.75 is back below the AVWAP, inside the unproven zone the bias line described.
2MNQ closes below 28,600.35 on 2 hourly closesNOT TRIGGEREDn/aLowest hourly close 28,777.75, session low 28,750.00. Never came near it.
3MES reclaims its 7,540.81 stop on 2 hourly closesNOT TRIGGEREDn/aHighest close of the day 7,523.50, 17.31 short. The bias line held exactly: MES stayed under its stop, MNQ stayed above its own, and neither trend read was clean all session.
4MGC holds above 4,000 on hourly closesTRIGGEREDMIXEDThe hold broke first. Two consecutive Sunday-open closes printed below at 3,998.50 and 3,999.70 and the overnight low ran 3,986.60, exactly the 2-close breach the bias warned about. Gold then reclaimed 4,000 and defended it on every close after, RTH low landing on 4,000.00 to the tick. Neither upside reference was reached: RTH high 4,023.90 stopped short of 4,029.
5MCL holds above the 80.83 stop on hourly closesTRIGGEREDMIXEDIt went up first, to 84.20 on the Sunday open, then fully reversed. The 11:00 UTC bar closed 80.25, below the level, and the overnight low ran 79.55. The stop flipped short on the 07:00 close and back long on the 18:00 close at 82.85. Closed 82.44. The bias warning about overnight repricing was right, in both directions.

Score: 5 rows, 3 triggered, 2 not triggered. 0 played out, 0 failed, 3 mixed. MTD: 42 triggered, 22 played out, 5 failed.

Calendar: Tuesday July 21 carries no major scheduled US release. The week's data lands Friday with July flash PMIs and June New Home Sales at 9:45 and 10:00 ET. The live variable is the earnings block: Alphabet, Tesla, Intel and IBM all report this week.

fullReport.title: Strategy Habitat Check
fullReport.html:
StrategyHabitatRead
AVWAP Hand-off (short only)IN_HABITATTextbook on MES. The opening range broke DOWN at 13:50 UTC at 7,528.50 with no reclaim and price trended below the NYVWAP to close 7,483.50. The MNQ version did not qualify: it broke down at 13:55 and reclaimed at 14:25, the filter working as designed.
TSMOM-20BORDERLINEA long signal exists on MNQ, MGC and MCL, but MNQ's is 15.34 points from invalidation and MES points the other way. MCL flipped twice inside the session. Signal present, quality poor.
AVWAP Deviation BandBORDERLINEMNQ closed at -1.60s, between bands rather than at one. The -2s sits at 28,743.08. Structure alive, no tag to act on at the close.
AVWAP MidrangeBORDERLINEThe MNQ opening range was wide at 156.25 points and price spent the 9:30 to 10:30 ET window inside it. Playable structure, wide risk.

Out of habitat today: AVWAP Bounce (MNQ lost the AVWAP into the close with no bounce, both index legs below it), AVWAP Back Into Range (fired on MNQ at 14:25 UTC but BIR is not eligible on Mondays, so it does not count), E3v2 Momentum Long (a distribution session with a broken index confirmation is the opposite of its regime filter).
